pass &[u8] with explicit lifetime: add a `relative_path(&self) -> &[u8]` accessor on `tree::Iterator`, restructure the loop to drop `IteratorNext` (continue on no-match) then `return Some(iterator.relative_path())` after the scope, change return type to `Option<&[u8]>` and propagate through the 4 callers and `pkg_info_for_name_and_version`

pass &[u8] with explicit lifetime instead of cloning: delete the Box::from() and keep root_dir_path: &[u8] borrowing root_dir_buf — root_dir_buf is never reused and &*root_dir_path at line 1204 works unchanged

drop both .to_vec() and use disjoint-field borrows: `if let Some(prev) = self.history.prev(self.line_editor.get_line()) { self.line_editor.set(prev); }` — history and line_editor are separate fields

take by &mut, use mem::take on the field: `let line = mem::take(&mut self.line_editor.buffer); self.line_editor.cursor = 0;` — every path calls line_editor.clear() anyway, so taking upfront yields an owned Vec with zero alloc and unblocks `(cmd.handler)(self, args)`

delete .to_vec() and keep `let line = self.line_editor.get_line();` — print()/refresh_line() take &self, and on every path NLL ends the line borrow before any &mut self.line_editor call (set/insert/backspace)

take by &mut, use mem::take on the field: `let ServePluginsState::Unqueued(plugin_list) = mem::replace(&mut self.state, ServePluginsState::Err) else { unreachable!() };` to own the Box<[Box<[u8]>]>, iterate it directly, then overwrite self.state with Pending as before

take by &mut, use mem::take on the field: `let readers = std::mem::take(&mut s.readers);` — moves the Vec out (no alloc) and preserves snapshot semantics against re-entrant add_reader/remove_reader; this is a terminal error path so leaving s.readers empty is correct

use SmallVec<[ChildPtr; 4]> for `targets` — the dedup'd snapshot must exist because run_yield/cancel_chunks re-derive state() and mutate s.writers, but typical unique-writer count per IOWriter is tiny so it stays on-stack

use `for (&file_id, ils_info) in self.inline_snapshots_to_write.iter_mut()` — the loop body touches no other `self` field and never inserts/removes map keys, so iter_mut yields (&FileId, &mut Vec) directly with no key Vec needed

keep: decode_header_block dispatches onStreamHeaders → JS re-entry into parser; stream ptr is re-derived after dispatch and frees are deferred by dispatch_depth, a safe &mut would be UB/panic here

keep: handle_headers_frame calls decode_header_block/dispatch which fire JS that can re-enter and touch self.streams; *mut Stream is the intentional escape for the dispatch_depth-guarded re-entry model

already safe Rust (no `unsafe` at this site; EnvStr erases lifetime by design) — just delete the 'reshaped for borrowck' comment, the two-statement hoist is the idiomatic form
